get.description:
Return the current user and all orgs they're a member of.

Side effect: on the first call for a brand-new user, this auto-provisions
their personal org + a starter API token. The starter token's plaintext
is included in this response **only on that first call** — subsequent
calls return ``starter_token: null``.

post.description:
Create a non-personal team org. The caller becomes its owner.

Note: this endpoint requires X-Org-Id of an existing org the user is
in (auth is org-context-scoped). The new org is created independently.

delete.description:
Soft-delete a team org with the full §2 cascade. Owner only.
Personal orgs cannot be deleted.

post.description:
Mint a new token. The plaintext value is in the response **once** —
after this call returns, only the prefix is ever shown.

delete.description: Idempotent. Re-revoking a revoked token returns the same row.

post.description:
Queue an agent run against a scenario.

Returns 202 immediately.  The caller may open the ``stream_url`` as a
Server-Sent Events endpoint to follow live progress, or poll
``GET /v1/runs/{run_id}`` for terminal state.

At most one active run per scenario is allowed at a time.  Idempotency
key support: supplying the same key twice returns the original run.

schema.title: Response List Runs V1 Runs Get

get.description:
SSE stream of normalised UI events for a run.

Three branches:

1. **Live channel** — orchestrator is currently emitting; subscribe and
   stream until ``None`` sentinel.
2. **No live channel + events.jsonl present in R2** — replay the file
   through ``normalise_event`` so a client that connected late still
   sees the full event log (typical when the run completed in seconds
   before the UI mounted).
3. **Nothing on disk either** — emit a single ``run.done`` so the
   client falls back to ``GET /runs/{id}`` for terminal state.

schema.title: Response List Runs By Product V1 Products  Product Id  Runs Get
Response List Runs By Scenario V1 Scenarios  Scenario Id  Runs Get

post.description:
Request cancellation of an active run.

Idempotent — terminal runs return their current state without error.
The runner checks the cancel signal on each stdout line and terminates
the Modal sandbox cleanly.

Create a new run with the same parameters as an existing terminal run.

The original run is not mutated.  Settings are re-resolved from the DB so
any changes since the original run (new skill content, updated agent
config) are picked up.  The frozen ``snapshot`` is NOT reused — this is a
fresh execution, not a clone.

Re-run the LLM judge on a completed run's existing transcript.

Returns 202 immediately; judge runs in the background and writes verdict
scalars to the run row.  Useful when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY wasn't set at run
time (heuristic stub) or after updating the judge model.

schema.title: Response Rejudge V1 Runs  Run Id  Rejudge Post

get.description: Stream the raw events.jsonl from R2/S3 for a completed run.

Return stderr.log from R2/S3 for a completed run.

Only present when the claude process wrote to stderr.  Returns 404 if the
run produced no stderr output.
Return analysis.json from R2/S3 for a completed run.

schema.title: Response Get Run Analysis V1 Runs  Run Id  Analysis Get

ApiTokenCreateResponse.title: ApiTokenCreateResponse
ApiTokenCreateResponse.description:
Response shape for POST /v1/api-tokens — includes the plaintext.

Shown exactly once at creation. Subsequent reads of the same row never
return the secret.

ApiTokenRead.title: ApiTokenRead
ApiTokenRead.description: Response shape for list/get — **never** includes the plaintext secret.

BasicAuthRef.title: BasicAuthRef
BasicAuthRef.description:
Reference to basic-auth credentials stored as secrets.

Both fields hold ``${secret:<name>}`` strings; resolved at run start.

InvitationCreateResponse.title: InvitationCreateResponse
InvitationCreateResponse.description:
Response shape for POST /v1/invitations.

``accept_url`` is included **only** when ``mode = "link"`` — for
``mode = "email"`` the URL is sent to the recipient instead. The URL
contains a one-time secret token; treat it like a password.

McpRef.title: McpRef
McpRef.description:
An MCP server reference. ``transport`` decides which other fields apply.

- ``http``: ``url`` is required; no ``command``/``args``.
- ``stdio``: ``command`` (+ optional ``args``) are required; no ``url``.

Per-MCP ``tool_allowlist`` restricts which exposed tools the agent may
invoke at runtime.

MemberRead.title: MemberRead
MemberRead.description:
Member metadata. ``user_id`` is the Neon Auth user UUID — handy for
UI personalization, but the source of truth for the user is Neon Auth.

OrgMembership.title: OrgMembership
OrgMembership.description: An org from the perspective of the calling user — includes their role.

ProductRead.title: ProductRead
ProductRead.description: Response shape. ``health`` is a derived rollup — see service layer.

RunCreated.title: RunCreated
RunCreated.description:
202 response — returned immediately after the run is queued.

The caller can stream progress from ``stream_url`` (SSE) or poll
``GET /v1/runs/{run_id}`` for the terminal state.

ScenarioRead.title: ScenarioRead
ScenarioRead.description:
Header + the *current* version's content, denormalized for one-call
UI consumption. Use the ``/versions`` endpoints to fetch a specific past
version.

ScenarioUpdate.title: ScenarioUpdate
ScenarioUpdate.description:
All fields optional. The service bumps ``current_version`` only when
a versioned field (``description_template`` / ``inputs`` /
``success_criteria``) actually changes. Header-only edits (name, slug,
default_agent_id) leave version history alone.

SkillRead.title: SkillRead
SkillRead.description: Single-item shape — includes the current version's content.

SkillReadMeta.title: SkillReadMeta
SkillReadMeta.description:
Cheap list-row shape — metadata only. Excludes ``content`` so list
responses stay small (mvp-api.md §10 List vs Get).
